applyTo: "docs/**,docs/roadmap.md"

# Flow Engineer Context — Auto-loaded

When editing planning documents you are working on the momentum architecture of the Somnatek ARG. Every change to the roadmap or design docs has downstream pacing consequences. Apply flow discipline.

## Current Phase 1 Progression Map

```
[somnatek.org public pages]
     ↓ robots.txt / HTML source / HTTP headers
[patient-resources.html] → five PDFs (cross-form cipher, acrostics, ghost text)
     ↓ metadata Subject chain → "When you find this room 413 is waiting"
[portal.html] → PTX-018 answer → VIS-XXXXX issued          [milestone: portal_solved 20pts]
     ↓
[portal/ptx-018/] → 22 session records                     [milestone: recall_accessed 25pts]
     ↓
[portal/protocol-7a/] → SHA-256 gate (15PTX018413)         [milestone: protocol_7a 30pts]
     ↓
[archive/correspondence/]                                   [milestone: correspondence_found 25pts]
[docs/7A_INTERNAL_DO_NOT_DISTRIBUTE.html]                   [milestone: doc_7a_found 15pts]
[archive/7A-SUPP-INDEX/] → redirect + HTML comment paths   [milestone: supp_index_found 20pts]
     ↓
[archive/7A-SUPP-010/] → Ellison access report             [milestone: supp_010_found 35pts]
[archive/7A-SUPP-005/] → termination reports               [milestone: supp_005_found 40pts]
     ↓
[admin/] → Tier 1 (HARROW-FAC)                             [milestone: admin_t1 10pts]
         → Tier 2 (7A-RC-2012)                             [milestone: admin_t2 20pts]
         → Tier 3 (PLEASE WAIT TO BE RECALLED)             [milestone: admin_t3 35pts]
         → Player's VIS ID in recall queue. Protocol 7A = ACTIVE.
```

**Released: 285 pts / 355 pts all-time. 11 milestones active.**

## Known Flow Problems (Phase 1)

| Problem | Location | Risk level |
|---|---|---|
| PDF form numbers → PTX IDs requires leap with no hard prompt | patient-resources → portal | Medium bottleneck |
| SUPP-010 and SUPP-005 only reachable via SUPP-INDEX HTML source | supp-index → supp-010/005 | High — requires view-source habit |
| Admin Tier 1 credential in `closure-notice.html` comment | closure-notice → admin | Low visibility |
| Fax line (morse `413`) not connected to a downstream step yet | fax → ??? | Orphaned signal |

## Phase Targets

| Phase | Status | Entry point | Finale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 — Somnatek | Live | somnatek.org public site | Admin Tier 3, player in recall queue |
| Phase 2 — RestWell | Not built | lortiz RestWell HTML comment | TBD — player feels watched |
| Phase 3 — Wexler | Not built | Wexler research abstract | TBD — player questions whether stopping is possible |
| Phase 4 — Harrow County | Not built | County business records | TBD |

## Pacing Rules

- Every phase needs at least two independent entry vectors at each depth level
- Progress must be visible — each milestone should feel like crossing a threshold
- No phase should end with an anti-climax
- Planned content drop gaps (daily/weekly/monthly) are pacing tools — treat silence as intentional pressure
- Each phase finale must be harder-earned and emotionally larger than the previous one

## Player Role Coverage Check

When designing a phase, confirm all five ARG role types have meaningful work:
- **Searcher** — finds entry vectors, scans source, checks headers
- **Pattern solver** — connects cross-document data (PDFs, dates, IDs)
- **Archivist** — builds timeline, tracks contradictions
- **Communicator** — coordinates community solves
- **Skeptic** — pressure-tests whether something is a real clue

## Example Prompts

- "Audit the Phase 1 progression map — where are the real bottlenecks and what parallel paths are missing"
- "The fax line morse clue is an orphaned signal — design a downstream step that connects it to the portal solve path without making the connection obvious"
- "Design the Phase 2 RestWell progression map — solo player 2–4 hours, community 30–60 min, finale makes the player feel watched"
- "What should the Phase 3 Wexler finale feel like and how do we make it harder-earned than the Admin Tier 3 reveal"
- "Design a community coordination puzzle for Phase 3 that requires Searcher, Pattern solver, and Archivist role types simultaneously"
